trim_reasons: PHI is the heavy favored side on a 14.5-point spread, meaning late-game rest for Maxey is a live risk even with the blowout penalty already applied. The iron_floor tag provides some protection, but the Jokic audit lesson confirmed that even iron_floor picks on historically large spreads produce Q4 minute compression — and 74% confidence leaves little margin if Maxey sits the fourth.

VOLATILE tag plus blowout game script (spread=-14.5, PHI favored) plus B2B rest combine on a non-iron-floor pick at minimum qualifying confidence (70%). The audit lesson is explicit: trim-but-keep at marginal confidence with VOLATILE tag and blowout risk concentrates exactly the picks that underperform, and the Barnes AST lesson shows the market pricing a near-certainty against these setups is a systemic caution signal.

B2B on a blowout-risk game where PHI is a heavy favorite creates real Q4 garbage-time risk; if Maxey sits the fourth, his 3PM attempts compress sharply. The pick is non-iron-floor at 74% with moderate volatility, so reduced shot volume late in a decided game is enough to swing a 2-make threshold.

B2B hit rate of 71% is the weakest B2B number on the slate and sits materially below the overall 85%, meaning the floor genuinely shifts today. Layering a 14.5-point blowout-risk spread on the favored side adds late-game minute compression risk for a role player whose assist production depends on playing meaningful minutes.

The FG_COLD step-down already flagged a cold-shooting pattern, and the pick lands at 74% non-iron-floor against a tough NY defense. The post_miss bounce-back rate of 69% (lift <1.0) is the weakest on the slate and explicitly below-average, meaning a sequential scoring slump is more likely here than a rebound — the structural floor is softer than the B2B hit rate suggests.

ORL is a 14.5-point favorite creating genuine Q4 rest risk for a non-iron-floor pick; the FG_MARGIN_THIN step-down (4%) already signaled scoring fragility, and the post_miss bounce-back rate of 71% with a typical miss of 4 points below threshold means a cold-shooting night on reduced fourth-quarter minutes easily pushes the actual below 10.

VOLATILE tag with FG_MARGIN_THIN (6%) against a tough DET defense at 74% non-iron-floor — this is structurally the most fragile PTS pick on the slate (matching the OG Anunoby audit profile: VOLATILE + vs_tough suppression + marginal confidence). The audit lesson explicitly flags that trim-but-keep VOLATILE picks at ≤75% with documented tough-defense fragility should be excluded.

This is an exact replay of the audited Jokic PTS T15 miss: VOLATILE scorer, blowout-risk spread (12.5), DEN as heavy favorite, 74% confidence cap — the prior session confirmed Jokic was pulled in Q4 under nearly identical conditions and missed by 1 point. The audit explicitly notes the iron_floor tag at T20 did not protect T15 from garbage-time minute reduction on a large spread, and the structural risk is not fully captured by the existing blowout cap.
